points.text: Genmab — Epcoritamab — Relapsed/Refractory Follicular Lymphoma — Presented EPCORE FL-1 subgroup data showing consistent efficacy and safety for epcoritamab (bispecific antibody) in combination with rituximab and lenalidomide (R2).

Genmab — Pipeline Strategy — Bispecific Antibodies — Confirmed advancement of bispecific antibodies as a core therapeutic format alongside ADCs and immune-modulating antibodies within its late-stage clinical pipeline.
Crescent Biopharma is advancing a clinical-stage PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody as its lead program for solid tumors alongside novel antibody-drug conjugates.

Genmab presented Phase 3 EPCORE FL-1 subgroup data at the 2026 EHA Congress showing epcoritamab, a subcutaneous T-cell engaging bispecific antibody, combined with rituximab and lenalidomide (R2) delivered consistent efficacy across relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma subgroups.

Post-hoc analysis of the pivotal Phase 3 EPCORE FL-1 trial (N=481) confirmed progression-free survival benefits for epcoritamab + R2 versus R2 alone with hazard ratios consistently below 0.3 across FLIPI scores, POD24 status, and patient fitness levels.

Cullinan Therapeutics — CLN-978 (CD19xCD3 T cell engager) — Systemic Lupus Erythematosus and Rheumatoid Arthritis — Initial Phase 1 data presented at EULAR 2026 showed clinical benefit including remissions after single target dose; deep, dose-dependent B cell depletion observed in peripheral blood and tissue.

Cullinan Therapeutics — CLN-978 (CD19xCD3 T cell engager) — Rheumatoid Arthritis — Favorable safety profile demonstrated with single target doses up to 30 µg; multi-dose regimen data from first cohort to be shared at Immunology Day on June 10.
Cullinan Therapeutics — Velinotamig (BCMAxCD3 T cell engager) — Autoimmune diseases — Initial clinical data scheduled for presentation at Cullinan's Immunology Day on June 10, 2026.
Merck and Akeso are competing in the lung cancer space where bispecific antibodies are gaining traction alongside antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs).

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab (KN026) — HER2-positive breast cancer — Phase 3 HEALER trial met primary endpoint of progression-free survival with significant risk reduction vs trastuzumab/pertuzumab/docetaxel; designed as a HER2 bispecific antibody.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab — HER2-positive gastric/gastroesophageal junction adenocarcinoma — China NMPA approved combination with chemotherapy in May 2026 for patients with 1 prior trastuzumab line.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab — Early/locally advanced HER2-positive breast cancer — Phase 3 Neo-Healer trial achieved 62.4% total pathological complete response vs 51.2% for standard THP regimen (absolute difference 11.4%).

Genmab — Epcoritamab — Relapsed/Refractory Follicular Lymphoma — EPCORE FL-1 subgroup analysis demonstrated consistent efficacy and safety results for the bispecific antibody epcoritamab when combined with rituximab and lenalidomide (R2).
Crescent Biopharma — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Solid tumors — Clinical-stage lead program advancing as single agent and in combination regimens.

Genmab — Epcoritamab + R2 — Relapsed or Refractory Follicular Lymphoma — Phase 3 EPCORE FL-1 subgroup analysis presented at EHA 2026: epcoritamab (subcutaneous T-cell engaging bispecific antibody) + R2 showed sustained efficacy and manageable safety with PFS hazard ratios <0.3 across all risk subgroups.

Cullinan Therapeutics — CLN-978 — Systemic Lupus Erythematosus / Rheumatoid Arthritis — Phase 1 study presented at EULAR 2026; single target dose induced remissions and deep B cell depletion; subcutaneous administration supported for outpatient use.
Cullinan Therapeutics — Velinotamig — Autoimmune diseases — BCMAxCD3 T cell engager program advancing; initial clinical data to be released June 10, 2026.
Merck / Akeso — Bispecific antibodies — Lung cancer — Competitive landscape analysis indicates both modalities are gaining traction; strategic alliance potential under review.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab (KN026) — HER2-positive breast cancer — Phase 3 HEALER trial (NCT05838066) met primary endpoint of PFS in 880-patient study comparing anbenitamab/docetaxel vs THP regimen.
CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab (KN026) — HER2-positive gastric cancer — China NMPA granted approval in May 2026 for combination with chemotherapy in second-line setting.
CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Anbenitamab (KN026) — Early HER2-positive breast cancer — Phase 3 Neo-Healer trial (NCT06747338) reported 62.4% tpCR vs 51.2% for THP control arm in neoadjuvant setting.

Genmab — Bispecific antibody commercialization and pipeline expansion — Epcoritamab (bispecific) + broader antibody portfolio — Advancing eight approved antibody medicines and a strong late-stage clinical pipeline including wholly owned bispecific programs.

Crescent Biopharma — Oncology pipeline expansion — PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody + ADCs — Clinical-stage; presenting at Jefferies Global Healthcare Conference (June 3, 2026) and Goldman Sachs 47th Annual Global Healthcare Conference (June 8, 2026).

Genmab — Bispecific antibody commercialization and pipeline expansion — Epcoritamab (bispecific) + R2 regimen — Phase 3 EPCORE FL-1 trial established fixed-duration therapy for R/R follicular lymphoma with consistent efficacy across high- and low-risk disease features.

Cullinan Therapeutics — Clinical-stage T cell engager development — CLN-978 (CD19xCD3) + Velinotamig (BCMAxCD3) — Phase 1 data presented June 2026; focused on disease-modifying treatments for autoimmune diseases and cancer.
Merck — Bispecific antibody competition in oncology — Lung cancer programs — Positioning against ADCs and other bispecifics in a rapidly evolving therapeutic area.
Akeso — Bispecific antibody development — Lung cancer — Expanding pipeline presence to compete with global players like Merck in the bispecific modality space.

CSPC Pharmaceutical Group — Commercialization of HER2 bispecific antibody — Anbenitamab (KN026) — Phase 3 data positive in breast cancer; NMPA approval secured for gastric cancer indication in May 2026.

Epcoritamab — Mechanism: Bispecific T-cell engager (implied by class) — Phase: Late-stage (EPCORE FL-1) — Population: Relapsed or Refractory Follicular Lymphoma — Comparator: Combination with Rituximab and Lenalidomide (R2) — Results: Consistent efficacy and safety results demonstrated in subgroup analysis.
PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Mechanism: Dual blockade of PD-1 and VEGF pathways — Phase: Clinical-stage — Population: Solid tumors — Strategy: Single agent and combination regimens.

Epcoritamab — Mechanism: Subcutaneous T-cell engaging bispecific antibody targeting CD20 and CD3 — Phase: 3 (EPCORE FL-1) — Population: Relapsed or refractory follicular lymphoma (N=481) — Comparator: Rituximab + lenalidomide (R2) — Results: PFS hazard ratios consistently below 0.3 across FLIPI 0–2 vs 3–5, POD24 vs non-POD24, and NHL-5 fitness subgroups.

CLN-978 — Mechanism: CD19xCD3 T cell engager — Phase: 1 — Population: Refractory SLE and difficult-to-treat RA patients — Comparator: None stated — Results: Clinical benefit including remissions; deep, dose-dependent B cell depletion in peripheral blood and tissue; safe up to 30 µg single dose.
Velinotamig — Mechanism: BCMAxCD3 T cell engager — Phase: 1 — Population: Autoimmune disease patients — Comparator: None stated — Status: Initial clinical data pending release June 10, 2026.

Bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: Dual-target engagement — Phase: Varies by program — Population: Lung cancer patients — Context: Emerging as a key modality competing with ADCs in oncology treatment.

Anbenitamab — Mechanism: HER2 bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 — Population: HER2-positive advanced breast cancer — Comparator: Trastuzumab/pertuzumab/docetaxel — Endpoints: Significant PFS improvement; trend toward OS benefit.

Anbenitamab — Mechanism: HER2 bispecific antibody — Phase: 3 (Neo-Healer) — Population: Early/locally advanced HER2-positive breast cancer — Comparator: THP regimen — Endpoints: 62.4% tpCR vs 51.2% (95% CI 3.19%-21.6% difference).
